reviewsSample.text: Such a charming bazaar, offering a wide range of authentic Iraqi items. You can find just about anything, and the indoor setting makes it perfect for escaping the hot sun.

The main Bazaar leading to the shrine of Imam Ali a.s. A vibrant and historic marketplace that offers an immersive shopping experience. This ancient bazaar, dating back centuries, is a treasure trove of traditional Iraqi goods, including jewelry, colorful textiles and fragrant spices. As you navigate the bustling alleys, you'll encounter friendly vendors, tantalizing street food, and a rich cultural atmosphere. A must-visit destination whilst in Najaf.
The shop owners aren't very pushy as you are walking by.

This is one of the best experience i had in Iraq.. the souk is usually crowded first of all but you will find everything you want. There are variety of options from food, books, souvenirs, clothing and jewellery. Anything you want is here my friend.. really historical place i love it.. i bought a book i was searching for and it was cheap, I also ate there which was amazing! But not all of the restaurants are hygienic so just make sure you are in a good spot.. i will visit again

The Grand Najaf Market is one of the most famous markets in the holy city of Najaf, located about 160 km south of Baghdad. It lies in the heart of the old city, directly opposite the shrine of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib, and extends to the Al-Midan area. The market offers a wide range of goods, including gold jewelry, fabrics, antiques, clothing, and traditional sweets. Several smaller markets known as Qaysariyas branch off from it, each specializing in specific products such as spices, leather goods, fabrics, and clothing.
